---
title: "Week 5: One-Way ANOVA"
date: "2026-09-14"
---

## Overview

This week bridges the gap between regression with dummy variables and the classical analysis of variance (ANOVA) framework. You will learn how the F-test partitions total variance into between-group and within-group components, how to test omnibus hypotheses about group mean differences, and how planned contrasts and post-hoc corrections let you ask targeted follow-up questions. Understanding ANOVA as a special case of regression deepens your ability to analyze categorical predictors.

## Learning Objectives

By the end of this week, you will be able to:

- Decompose total variance into between-group and within-group sums of squares
- Construct and interpret the F-statistic for testing omnibus group differences
- Apply planned contrasts and effect coding to test specific hypotheses about group means
- Implement Bonferroni and other multiple-comparison corrections to control familywise error
- Explain the equivalence between one-way ANOVA and regression with categorical predictors

## Readings

- {{< fa book-open >}} **Required**: [Chapter 3: One-Way ANOVA]({{< var books.lecture_notes >}}/chapters/03-one-way-anova.html){target="_blank"}

## R Lab

- {{< fa laptop-code >}} [Lab 04: Categorical Predictors and Contrasts]({{< var books.r_lab_book >}}/labs/lab04-categorical-contrasts.html){target="_blank"}

## Homework

- {{< fa pen-ruler >}} [Homework 03](/assignment/hw03.qmd) — Due this week
- {{< fa pen-ruler >}} [Homework 04](/assignment/hw04.qmd) — Due this week

## Quiz

- {{< fa circle-question >}} **Quiz 2** — Covers Weeks 3--5 material

## Key Concepts

| Concept | Description |
|---------|-------------|
| **One-way ANOVA** | A method for testing whether the means of three or more groups differ significantly |
| **F-statistic** | The ratio of between-group variance to within-group variance used to test the omnibus null hypothesis |
| **Between-group variance** | Variability in the outcome attributable to differences among group means |
| **Within-group variance** | Variability in the outcome attributable to individual differences within groups |
| **Planned contrasts** | Focused comparisons among group means specified before examining the data |
| **Bonferroni correction** | An adjustment that divides the significance level by the number of comparisons to control familywise error |
| **Effect coding** | A coding scheme where group membership is coded so that the intercept represents the grand mean |
